summary: Chromium browser credential stealer shipped as V8 bytecode with bundled App-Bound key extractor
details: On require/load, this package executes a Chromium-family credential stealer against Windows hosts. The main entry is a 3-line stub that loads opaque V8 bytecode via bytenode (index.js -> require('./index.jsc')); the license module is also shipped as bytecode (src/license.jsc). No JavaScript source is provided for the actual functionality. The bytecode kills running browsers (taskkill /F /IM...), copies Cookies and Login Data SQLite databases from Chrome, Brave, and Edge user profile directories, and decrypts saved passwords via 'SELECT origin_url, username_value, password_value FROM logins'. A bundled 680KB Windows x64 native Node addon at build/Release/debugelevator.node performs Chrome App-Bound (v20) encryption key extraction (extractMasterKey / app_bound_encrypted_key / appBoundKey, '[*] Starting debug session...') consistent with COM IElevator/AppBoundEncryptionService abuse used to defeat Chrome v127+ credential protection. Decrypted cookies and passwords are written to local _cookies/ and _passwords/ directories. The license gate in src/license.jsc fetches https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tabo1337/SaatPCBKod/refs/heads/main/key.txt (mutable main branch on a GitHub account unrelated to the npm publisher), giving the operator an external kill-switch / dynamic-config channel. package.json carries deceptive metadata (description 'hadiyapic', placeholder repository 'github.com/yourusername/baksen.git', no README) so a developer cannot learn the true purpose before install. The combination of bytecode-only delivery, deceptive metadata, a bundled native Chrome elevation helper, and a credential-theft pipeline is an unambiguous infostealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
